chapter_context.summary: Records the dramatic confrontation with the false prophet Hananiah, who breaks Jeremiah's wooden yoke and is rebuked with a iron yoke and sudden death.

outline.text: Hananiah's False Prophecy of Two-Year Exile Deliverance
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hananiah's False Prophecy of Two-Year Exile Deliverance (28:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Jeremiah's Rebuttal: True Prophets Prophesy Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeremiah's Rebuttal: True Prophets Prophesy Judgment (28:5-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Hananiah Breaks Yoke; Prophesying Iron Yokes and Death
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hananiah Breaks Yoke; Prophesying Iron Yokes and Death (28:10-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 28, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
